attempts.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
    
        The image of the scene has been provided.
        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Inspect a book from the bed with a light.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
The scene is a bedroom containing several objects relevant to the planning task. The primary focus is on a bed, a nightstand, and objects placed on them.

**Objects and Types:**
- **Receptacles**: The nightstand is a wooden piece of furniture with a drawer, which qualifies as a receptacle. It is openable.
- **Objects**: 
    - A book with a blue cover and a white sheep illustration on its front. It is resting on the bed.
    - A laptop with a silver casing and a visible logo on its lid. It is also on the bed.
    - A lamp with a green lampshade and a pink base, placed on the nightstand. The lamp is an object that can be toggled.
    - Two white, tufted pillows are on the bed.
    - A yellow pencil and a small glass jar containing red and blue items are on the nightstand.

**Quantities:**
- There is one nightstand (receptacle).
- There is one book (object).
- There is one laptop (object).
- There is one lamp (object).
- There are two pillows (objects, not relevant to domain).
- There is one pencil (object, not relevant to domain).
- There is one glass jar (object, not relevant to domain).

**Spatial Relationships:**
- The book is on the bed, positioned to the left of the laptop.
- The laptop is on the bed, centered between the two pillows.
- The two pillows are on the bed, one to the left and one to the right of the laptop.
- The lamp is on the nightstand, which is positioned to the right of the bed.
- The pencil and glass jar are on the nightstand, next to the lamp.
- The nightstand is adjacent to the bed, on the right side.

**Current State of Objects:**
- The book is not being held by any agent and is not in any receptacle.
- The laptop is not being held by any agent and is not in any receptacle.
- The lamp is not toggled on (it is off).
- The nightstand drawer is closed (not opened).

**Visual Properties:**
- The bed has a wooden frame and a white mattress with a light-colored patterned bedspread.
- The nightstand is made of light brown wood with a single drawer and a gold-colored knob.
- The lamp has a green, pleated lampshade and a pink base with a black circular ring.
- The book has a blue cover with a white sheep illustration and gold lettering.
- The laptop is silver with a small, circular logo on the lid.
- The pillows are white with button tufting.
- The pencil is yellow, and the glass jar contains small red and blue objects.

The robot needs to interact with the book and the lamp to inspect the book with light. The lamp must be toggled on to provide illumination, and the book must be picked up or the robot must be positioned near it to inspect it. The nightstand is a receptacle that may need to be opened if the robot needs to retrieve or store items, but it is currently closed.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem inspect_book_with_light)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        bed - receptacle
        nightstand - receptacle
        book - object
        laptop - object
        lamp - object
        pillow1 - object
        pillow2 - object
        pencil - object
        jar - object
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation robot nightstand) ; Assuming robot starts near nightstand for efficiency
        (openable nightstand)
        (not (opened nightstand))
        (not (isOn lamp))
        (inReceptacle book bed)
        (inReceptacle laptop bed)
        (inReceptacle pillow1 bed)
        (inReceptacle pillow2 bed)
        (inReceptacle lamp nightstand)
        (inReceptacle pencil nightstand)
        (inReceptacle jar nightstand)
        (not (holdsAny robot))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (isOn lamp)
        (atLocation robot book)
    ))
)
```
attempts.problem:
(define (problem inspect_book_with_light)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        bed - receptacle
        nightstand - receptacle
        book - object
        laptop - object
        lamp - object
        pillow1 - object
        pillow2 - object
        pencil - object
        jar - object
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation robot nightstand) ; Assuming robot starts near nightstand for efficiency
        (openable nightstand)
        (not (opened nightstand))
        (not (isOn lamp))
        (inReceptacle book bed)
        (inReceptacle laptop bed)
        (inReceptacle pillow1 bed)
        (inReceptacle pillow2 bed)
        (inReceptacle lamp nightstand)
        (inReceptacle pencil nightstand)
        (inReceptacle jar nightstand)
        (not (holdsAny robot))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (isOn lamp)
        (atLocation robot book)
    ))
)
attempts.plan:
(gotolocation robot lamp)
(toggleobject robot lamp)
(gotolocation robot book)

(define (problem inspect_book_with_light)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        bed - receptacle
        nightstand - receptacle
        book - object

        (not (opened nightstand))
        (not (isOn lamp))

        (inReceptacle laptop bed)
        (inReceptacle pillow1 bed)
        (inReceptacle pillow2 bed)
        (inReceptacle lamp nightstand)
        (inReceptacle pencil nightstand)
        (inReceptacle jar nightstand)
        (not (holdsAny robot))

        robot - agent

        nightstand - receptacle

        laptop - object

        jar - object

        (atLocation robot nightstand) ; Assuming robot starts near nightstand for efficiency

        (not (opened nightstand))
        (not (isOn lamp))
        (inReceptacle book bed)
        (inReceptacle laptop bed)
        (inReceptacle pillow1 bed)
        (inReceptacle pillow2 bed)
        (inReceptacle lamp nightstand)
        (inReceptacle pencil nightstand)
        (inReceptacle jar nightstand)
        (not (holdsAny robot))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (isOn lamp)
        (atLocation robot book)
    ))
